entry.title: Re: Dynamic Date Range Selector in SAC Story based on Dat...
entry.content: I have come to conclusion that for this case, table created based on live data model can be achieved using variables on top of DS model

entry.title: Re: SAP Business One Create Invoice using SL but got a NotFound error
entry.content:
Hi mMason1,

Can you confirm if you have the Approval Process enabled?

Kind regards,

ANKIT CHAUHAN

SAP Business One Support

entry.title: Re: How do I upload an Excel File to Enable Now
entry.content:
I wouldn't upload the Excel file to SAP Enables Now, instead, I'd create a book page and add the HTML Document macro there with an embedded link to the Excel spreadsheet stored in its "natural habitat" (SharePoint or Onedrive).

Then you can assign the book page or a book with that book page to Learners.

Also, as the link to the Excel spreadsheet is permanent, you don't need to worry about the file updates.

entry.title: Re: Issue: Adaptation Project with Fiori Elements Flexible Programming Model (FPM)
entry.content: Hi, Did you find any solution? I am also facing the same issue.

entry.title: Re: SD_COND_ACCESS - performance S/4 vs ECC
entry.content: Check SAP note 3350691 assuming, you have already gone through the explanation suggesed by SAP here

entry.title: Re: SAC Planning Story unable add comments in View Mode
entry.content:
By saying not able to enter comment in view mode, what's the behavior? No popup for comment entering on double click? 

I'm seeing error message in the table detail info area up left corner. Maybe you should have a look as well. 

Or you should create a ticket to SAP on this.

entry.title: Re: Linking Documentation to Start and End Page
entry.content:
<p><a href="javascript:ctx.cfg_show('$#{project:.uid}:hands_on_document.pdf',false, null)"><img width="206" height="206" src="media!M_3FCC6600D654F48F:image.png"></a></p>

This is a source code for the "generic link" to a document. Create the active area, that covers the "link area" and paste the code there. 

The media object in the code represents the transparent image file inside the active area.

entry.title: Re: Seamless planning integration between SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere - product FAQ
entry.content:
Hi @Max_Gander 

Thanks for the workable temporary solution! I understand that some adjustment is required for some Datasphere objects after hierarchy is supported in HY2.2025 release.

Best regards,

Ricky

entry.title: Re: Setting up reporting in SAP Enable Now
entry.content:
Easy fix. 

Find the Report Viewer role, add the permission object "View: Reports with Personal Data" to this role. 

Assign the modified role to yourself or anyone else who needs these reports. Done.

If you try to add this permission object to the Administrators role, it won't work. 😂🤣

entry.title: Assistance Required: Restricting Receipt Stock Usage Before Production Order Closure -SAP B1 Busines
entry.content:
Dear All,

I need your assistance regarding the Production Order Receipt Stock.
My query is: How can we restrict sales users from using the receipt stock before the production order is closed as per the planned quantity?

Alternatively, is there a way to block this stock from being used until the production order is fully completed and closed?

Looking forward to your suggestions.

entry.title: Re: Job Application still shows on home page however JR c...
entry.content: thanks @karthick_0905 do you know if someone is an internal candidate & was in progress Job req however the Job Req was closed before hiring the internal candidate on the requisition. here is the link to KBA 3092691 - Job Application progress not synchronizing with "For You Today" New Home Page card - Recruiting Management

entry.title: Re: Every time I try to multiply two different accounts i...
entry.content: If your SAC tenant is NOT on HANA Cloud, you won't be able to get parent value via  RESULTLOOKUP(Parent). You should find other way to get parent value in this case.

entry.title: Re: Page Map error
entry.content: Can anyone provide some insight on this topic? I'm interested because I've encountered similar situations. If anyone has a solution or suggestions, please let me know. Thanks!

entry.title: Re: Concat two dimension properties and pass it to booked...
entry.content:
Concat is NOT yet supported in advance formula. 

Please vote and track enhancement: https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/284232

entry.title: Why AW01N show negative Net Book Value
entry.content:
Dear All,

i am wondering why does AW01N show negative Net Book Value?

where can i find the actual net book value for a asset?

Example:

FY2019
Asset Acquisition Value: 279,245
Ordinary deprec.: 4,733-

Unplanned dep.: 9,355-

Net Book Value: 265,157

FY2025
Acquisition Value - Accu.Depreication = Net Book Value

find attached file.

Thanks,

RTN

entry.title: Re: Fixed Assets Migration - Asset Value Date and Depreci...
entry.content:
Dear Ayman,

The cumulated values tab are referring to prior year values, therefore the default Asset value date when doing mid-year transfer is always the first day of the Fiscal year.

Please refer to the  Asset Accounting: Transaction Types.xlsx (SAP Customer) for more transaction type. For example, if you want to post a retirement without revenue, you can use transaction type (TTY) 200.

Best Regards,

Evelyn

entry.title: Re: Selective Data Transition with Time Slice.
entry.content: Informative

entry.title: Re: Every time I try to multiply two different accounts i...
entry.content: I don't think that is true: I can display the value of the Leaf divided by the Parent, just the leaf and just the parent. What does not work is the combination of various values.

entry.title: SAP Cold Data Management - BW on HANA and ADLS Integration via SAP Datasphere or BDC
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community members, 

We are planning to move the cold data storage from On-prem SAP BW on HANA to cost-effective Azure Data Lake Storage (ADLS). So currently we are exploring various options to perform this functionality. So please provide the best and recommended option to achieve this functionality.

fyi, in our landscape we already have On-Prem SAP BW on HANA, SAP Datasphere and SAP Analytics Cloud applications and also plan to have a BDC tenant subscription.

entry.title: Re: Enable Now Manager LDAPS - Unsupported signature algo...
entry.content:
Hi Alec,

Sorry, I don't have an answer for you other than to get you to raise an SAP support ticket using the Component ID KM-SEN-MGR and get support involved.  I'm sure that you will be able to resolve your issue.

Regards
Shane

entry.title: Re: Multiple Billing Document Attachments in Single Email...
entry.content:
Hello,

This requirement looks close enough to the "billing lists" functionnality of standard SAP. 
You can set up automatic daily billing list generation the would regroup all invoices of the day and then set up input generation on your billing list document directly. That output would enable you to automate sending a single mail with a PDF detail of all invoices at once.
But that would still require additionnal work from a developper, as the context and variables readily available changes slightly.
Regards.

entry.title: Re: Restrict additional LOIPRO IDOC through BADI
entry.content: Make use of a status selection profile which only includes the relevant statussen for which you do want to send out an idoc and which specifically excludes the unwanted statusses and assign it to the variant you use for RCCLORD.

entry.title: Re: SAP Signavio April 2025 Release: SAP Signavio Process Insights and SAP Signavio Process Intellig
entry.content: Here you can find five demos which show the Process Insights improvement opportunities in financials, sales, procurement and warehouse management: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/2505-2504-release-highlights-of-sap-signavio-process-insights/ba-p/14110035

entry.title: Re: Chart_1.getDataSource().getDimensionFilters() not ret...
entry.content: getDimensionfitler() can only get filter directly applied to the chart, to get filter applied through input control, you need to use InputControl_1.getInputControlDataSource().getActiveSelectedMembers().

entry.title: Case Study: Host Agent using high CPU when connecting to multiple Focused Run and Solution Manager
entry.content:
In this blog post I would like to share a case of High CPU usage by the SAP Host Agent on Linux when connecting to multiple SAP Focused Run and SAP Solution Manager. After a thorough investigation lasting three months, the problem was successfully resolved.

Background
Outside Discovery serves as an important data supplier to the System Landscape Directory (SLD) and Landscape Management Database (LMDB). It reports critical information such as Database details, Host specifics, Simple Diagnostics Agent data, Microsoft IIS, and other XML-formatted payload files.

For SAP Solution Manager systems, Outside Discovery Diagnostics Agent (OD DAA) is supported by default. However, switching to the newer Outside Discovery SAP Host Agent (OD SHA) is recommended due to limitation in the older OD DAA mechanism, achievable by following SAP Note 2556432. 

In contrast, SAP Focused Run systems exclusively support the Outside Discovery SAP Host Agent, as outlined in the FRUN Preparing Outside Discovery Guide.

Problem Statement:
A high CPU usage issue was observed with SAP Host Agent on the server, seriously impacting server performance. The top command revealed that users sapadm and daaadm were consuming substantial resources.

Solution

  1. Clear the data flow from the managed server to the Focused Run or Solution Manager. Make sure there is no configuration loop.

    A configuration loop as the following example increases the SAP Host Agent workload and it has no meaning. A clear indication is seeing error message "[SLD Data Supplier Forwarding] ...429 Too Many Requests" in the /usr/sap/hostctrl/work/ousidediscovery.log. Note 0003082196 explains this error further. The 429 http response code is only returned by LMDB if we are getting two payloads from the same data supplier within a short time range. 

    The solution for the following example is to remove the "FRUN on Cloud" from the SLDR1 outbound connection. Alternatively, remove the SLDR2 destination file from Host Agent /usr/sap/hostctrl/exe/config.d. See the Preparing System Landscape Data Router for more information.

  2. Enable only the OD SHA for SAP Host Agent.

    Avoiding using the OD DAA to supply the Solution Manager, while using OD SHA to supply the Focused Run. Switching to the OD SHA in the Solution Manager Agent Administration as steps in SAP Note 2556432. It's suggested to integrate the Solution Manager link into the SLDR outbound connection to further reduce SAP Host Agent workload.

By following these steps, the high CPU usage issue was resolved, optimizing server performance and enhancing data reporting reliability.

entry.title: Re: SolMan 7.2 SPS 20 ACP file 63 not found
entry.content:
Hi Max,

Could you please find the file in software download center -> Search it with key word "ST-PI" -> Click Tab "Available to Download"?

https://me.sap.com/softwarecenter

Please make sure that your S-user has enough authorizations to access it.

Best regards,

Leo

SAP Support

entry.title: Re: SolMan 7.2 SPS 20 ACP file 63 not found
entry.content: Hi Max, what does the requesting screen look like? Could you please attach a screenshot? Best regards, Vera Jiang

entry.title: Submission aus Anfrage wird nicht in Bestellung übernommen
entry.content:
Hallo,

wenn wir eine Submissionsnummer in der Anfrage eintragen, wird diese bei der Bestellanlage aus dem Angebot nicht übernommen. Was muss hier eingestellt werden.

Danke für Ihre Rückmeldung

Viele Grüße

Robert Haller

entry.title: Re: SAP B1: Duplicated Windows in ALT+TAB
entry.content: I am experiencing same issue, Windows Feature Experience Pack 1000.26100.84.0

entry.title: Re: CDS view for revenue forecast in sap pubcli cloud
entry.content:
Hi Shahana,

Could you check if CDS View C_EngmntProjCostQuery (CDS Views: Engagement Project Cost and Revenue Query) and C_EngmtProjMargAnlysQuery(CDS Views: Engagement Project Margin Analysis Query) can be leveraged to fulfill the business need?

entry.title: BW on Hana data redistribution after adding a new node.
entry.content:
We have added a new node to the existing cluster for BW PERSON_NAME on Prem cluster. We don't see any data on the 4th Node. We would like to know if there is an additional process that is needed to redistribute the data or if any other steps needs to be performed. Also, if there any SAP Notes on this, please provide us the same.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: BW on Hana data redistribution after adding a new node.
entry.content:
You need a redistribution when adding a new node, and this mentioned in note:

3570289 - Is table redistribution needed after adding a host?

You could follow this note (depends on environment) to perform the redistribution.

1908075 - SAP BW on SAP HANA: Table Placement and Landscape Redistribution
2334091 - SAP BW/4HANA: Table Placement and Landscape Redistribution

entry.title: Re: Every time I try to multiply two different accounts i...
entry.content: I'm just pointing out one possibility. If you say you can get data from parent already, then you need to check if the driver is on the same data granularity as the original value.

entry.title: Re: Every time I try to multiply two different accounts i...
entry.content: It should be. The strange this is that until 3 days ago it all worked, and since (at least) yesterday it does not anymore. I have not changed anything. But as soon as I try the calculation it all jumps to 0.

entry.title: Re: Every time I try to multiply two different accounts i...
entry.content: My main goal is to use the disaggregation weights from another year to be able to break down the newly calculated Account value to its leaves based on them.

entry.title: Re: clean up the table GRACUSERPRMVL
entry.content:
Which parameters did you input at selection-screen of report GRAC_DELETE_VIOLATION_DATA

Did you adjust the SPRO parameters before rerun batch risk analysis?

  • Read KBA 2270608 - Size of table GRACUSERPRMVL impacts performance

entry.title: Re: My Journey to Becoming SAP Certified: From Missed Opportunity to TS410 Success
entry.content:
Hey @F_M,
Thanks for taking up the courage to share your journey to all of us. It is really very inspiring to me.
Am a similar crossroads like you where in 2023, I have just decided to step into the SAP ecosystem.
I would ideally like to have you as my mentor and connect with you so I can understand the nuances of sailing through in this SAP journey.
I really would like to connect with you both professionally and personally to guide me.
Would it be possible if its not too much to ask of you? If so, please let me know how we can connect further.
Thanks in advance for your support and understanding.

Best Regards
Arun

entry.title: Re: Large Volume of Data Replication – SAP S/4HANA to SAP...
entry.content: It is not black and white but I would suggest hotfolder as better approach for large data volumes compared to the Integration API that is used by integration flows available for Cloud Integration.

entry.title: Re: How to fetch Country of Origin from MARA to MCH1 in b...
entry.content: Hi Raymond, I should comment on your latest feedback however comment is not available. Would you kindly share with me the Syntax for this purpose in CU01?

entry.title: Re: Error C 056 “Field overflow during price calculation”...
entry.content: Check SAP note 1657220

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP Trial Account Creation and Enabling Integration Suite service(SAP CPI)
entry.content:
I also had issues with the Integration Suite and this might help someone.
Menu entries like "Configure" were missing, and the "Manage APIs" tile was inactive, even though I had all the required permissions.
I cleared the cache, logged out and back in, but nothing changed.

After two days, I randomly clicked through the menu again. When I clicked on "Settings", the system suddenly displayed a message saying it was being provisioned.
I was logged out automatically, and after logging back in, everything was fully available.

entry.title: Re: 外注について
entry.content:
Machine translation:

About Subcontracting

About Subcontracting (BMY)
Supplier ⇒ If you make a direct shipment of raw materials to the processing location,
(1) Is there a way to account for the special stock at B processing location instead of posting it to the company asset once?
(2) What will happen to the master setting (purchasing info record master etc.) to do so that the delivery destination of purchase orders generated by MRP is B processing target?

entry.content:
Dear Experts,

 

May I know if there is a way to query sum of Linetotal of sales order details in FMS, If we have a way to manipulate the data.

 

 

TIA,

entry.title: Re: New Standard Element of Company
entry.content: Thank you this does.

entry.title: Re: BAPI to remove ship-to from business partner
entry.content: Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Configure a tile on SAP Build Work Zone to manage Decision artifacts
entry.content:
Hi,

The issue I mentioned above is resolved after adding appropriate parameters in the application tile on Build Work Zone. Thanks! 

I have one query, as we are adding the project identifier as a parameter to the application does that mean at a time a Studio Home application can manage decisions for one project only.

We have multiple projects with rules spread across all projects due to the large rule data volume and SAP limitation on rule volume.

Does that mean I have to create multiple studio home application tiles i.e. one for every project?

Please advise.

Thanks,

Smita Patil

entry.title: Re: Empowering business to manage Decisions in SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content:
Hi,

One query, as we are adding the project identifier as a parameter to the application does that mean at a time a Studio Home application can manage decisions for one project only?

We have multiple projects with rules spread across all projects due to the large rule data volume and SAP limitation on rule volume.

Does that mean I have to create multiple studio home application tiles i.e. one for every project?

Please advise.

Thanks,

Smita Patil

entry.title: Re: How to fetch Country of Origin from MARA to MCH1 in b...
entry.content: Something like $SELF.V_XXXX=$PARENT.V_XXXX

entry.title: はじめてのSAP Analytics Cloud BI - シリーズまとめ
entry.content:
本ブログシリーズはSAP Analytics CloudのBI機能を使ってダッシュボードやレポートの作成を行う方を対象とし、SAP Analytics CloudのBIにおける基本の操作やTipsをハンズオン形式でご紹介します。

ブログは随時追加予定です。（最終更新 ‐ 2025/05/28）
是非、このページをブックマークして、定期的にチェックしていただけると嬉しいです。

※SAP Analytics Cloudの基本操作やSAP Analytics Cloudに関する内容、planning機能紹介シリーズをチェックしたい場合には、併せて以下のブログもご覧ください。
・SAP Analytics Cloud関連ブログまとめ
・入門！SAP Analytics Cloud for planning 機能紹介シリーズまとめ

はじめに！

  • 用語入門：SAP Analytics Cloudの基礎的な用語の紹介（今後追加予定）
  • サンプルモデル／ストーリーの作成：以下ブログにおけるハンズオンのベースとなるデータモデル、レポートの作成（2025/05/28）

チャートをよりリッチにしよう！

  • 差異の表示：予実差異や前年度差異を表示（今後追加予定）
  • しきい値の設定：しきい値の設定による色分け（今後追加予定）
  • 基準線の挿入：平均などの基準線の挿入（今後追加予定）
  • 計算：ストーリー上での簡単な計算設定（今後追加予定）

表示するデータを切り替えよう！

  • フィルタ／切り替えボタンの作成：フィルタリング、数値項目・分析軸の切り替えボタン（今後追加予定）
  • 動的テキストの追加：選択した項目に応じて変化するテキスト（今後追加予定）
  • リンク付き分析の設定：チャートのクリックによる他のチャートへの同時フィルタリング（今後追加予定）

より綺麗なストーリーにしよう！

  • デザインのTips：ストーリーを綺麗にするためのTips（今後追加予定）

ストーリーが完成したら共有しよう！

  • ストーリーの共有：SAP Analytics Cloud内での共有（今後追加予定）
  • 外部へのエクスポート：SAP Analytics Cloud外への共有（今後追加予定）

分析のための便利な機能！

  • データアナライザ：アドホックな分析機能（今後追加予定）
  • スマートインサイト：機械学習による分析サポート（今後追加予定）
  • Just Ask：自然言語による問い合わせ（今後追加予定）
  • ブックマーク：ユーザーが設定したフィルタ条件などカスタマイズした状態を保存（今後追加予定）

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA 2022 FPS1 Fully-Activated Appliance standard installation
entry.content:
@ashwini007  the error info show your APP link your db error.

please check hdbuserstrore info

su - s4hadm

>hdbuserstore list

like as :

then check R3trans -d

entry.title: Re: Production Order Confirmation Locking
entry.content:
Hi @adnanmaqbool,

Are you trying to do production order confirmation in mass?

If so, You can try out below options.

1) Using API ‘Production Order Confirmation’ (communication Scenario: Manufacturing Execution - Order and Confirmation Integration (SAP_COM_0522)).

https://api.sap.com/api/API_PROD_ORDER_CONFIRMATION_2_SRV/resource/Material_Movements_for_Confirmation

2) The other way is to perform mass confirmation of production order using app 'Mass Processing Produciton Order'. 

Using this way, it allows to confirm production orders in mass but doesn’t support excel upload to get the confirmation data.

Input the filter criteria and click on ‘Execute’

It displays the available list of production orders in the SAP S/4HANA cloud system.

By using ‘Mass Processing > Settings > select Function Confirmation > Adopt > Execute’, all the listed orders can be confirmed in mass. It displays the error log if any of the orders are not confirmed.

Thanks and Regards,

Prabha A

entry.title: Re: Every time I try to multiply two different accounts i...
entry.content: Hi @Kerstin8 , have you tried the tracing feature? Especial the table-feature can show you the data read by the RESULTLOOKUP operation: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ANALYTICS_CLOUD/00f68c2e08b941f081002fd3691d86a7/0767009b49d141c58fcc2962d6584b44.html

entry.title: SAP Free tier limitations for Integration suite
entry.content:
Hi,

Referring to the SAP Note: https://me.sap.com/notes/2903776.

Need few information's regarding the Free Tier Service Plan for SAP Cloud Integration.

1. How many interfaces can be developed in the Free Tier Service Plan?

2. Does the Free Tier support Best Effort Communication mode (Synchronous Communication)?

3. What is the message volume that is permitted in the free tier service plan?

4. What are the other limitations in using the free tier service plan ?

Regards,

Senthil

entry.title: Re: Functional Area Hierarchy in Group Financial Statemen...
entry.content:
Thanks Hengye,

We have genuine FA hierarchy build and in use already, but it's not available/reachable from Group Financial Statements Review booklet (just let me know if I misunderstood you point).

At the moment we are building Reporting Rules to have FA Hierarchy available also on that booklet (Hierarchy for FS Item and Reporting Rules to direct/filter proper Functional Area Sub items to correct Nodes). 

Any other clever ideas always welcome.

entry.title: getting "500 internal server error" while selecting the bucket in Portfolio Management
entry.content: getting "500 internal server error" while selecting the bucket in Portfolio Management in SAP PPM. What is the possible cause

entry.title: Re: RECN entries in SVT confirmed table
entry.content:
Hi Christoph,

Thank you so much for the suggestions and references to the threads provided.  I agree, it has reference to one of the threads created by me which had similar requirement I have put earlier. However, this time we are looking for entire customized report in one dashboard for confirmed qty.  We had already activated  BADI_EHSRC_TRACK_PROC  in Sandbox earlier but it  also needs activation of BF  LOG_EHS_CI_2 ,  and we want to avoid that in other environment due to effects on CG02 report management history display.  Besides, after activating the given BADI, we were able to view only one item at a time. That means, in CBRC20 we need to click  only one item of consolidated confirmed qty to drill down  for detailed confirmed qty history for that substance.

There is a program called RREGCH_EVALUATE_QUANTITIES .  Passing necessary variants there, one can get the Material document and substance information in one screen, but it also uses one material at a time.

Exploring further on this topic.

Regards,

Ashish

entry.title: はじめてのSAP Analytics Cloud BI - サンプルモデル／ストーリーの作成
entry.content:
本ブログシリーズはSAP Analytics CloudのBI機能を使ってダッシュボードやレポートの作成を行う方を対象とし、SAP Analytics CloudのBIにおける基本の操作やTipsをハンズオン形式でご紹介します。

本ブログでは、ブログシリーズでご紹介する内容をハンズオン形式で同様な操作を行えるよう、ベースとなるサンプルモデル／ストーリーの作成方法をご紹介します。
あくまでもブログシリーズのための簡易的なモデル／ストーリーの作成のため、より細かいモデルの設定や地図を含むストーリーの作成を学びたい方は、以下のブログをご覧ください。
触ってみよう！SAP Analytics Cloud－第1回：モデルの作成 - SAP Community
触ってみよう！SAP Analytics Cloud－第2回：ストーリーの作成 - SAP Community

今回はチャート3つ、テーブル1つの簡易的なストーリーを作成していきます。

※ SAP Analytics Cloudは、四半期に１度のペースで製品のバージョンアップが実施されます。そのため今後のバージョンアップにより、本ブログで紹介する画面キャプチャや、操作が異なる可能性があることをご了承ください。（本ブログは2025年5月に作成しています。）

1. サンプルデータのダウンロード

1.1. 以下のリンクにアクセスします。
サンプルデータのダウンロード

1.2. ダウンロードボタンをクリックして、エクセルファイルをダウンロードします。

2. モデルの作成

2.1. ナビゲーションバーの「モデラ」をクリックし、新規作成にある「モデル」を選択します。

2.2. 「データから開始」が選択されているのを確認し、「次へ」をクリックします。

2.3. 「ファイル（ローカルファイルまたはフ...」をクリックします。

2.4. 「ソースファイルの選択」より1でダウンロードしたエクセルファイルを選択し、「インポート」をクリックします。


2.5. モデルの編集画面が開きます。
ディメンションの「月」をクリックし、右の詳細パネルにてタイプを「一般」から「日付」に変更します。 

2.6. ディメンションの名前を「Date」に変更し、「OK」をクリックします。

2.7. ディメンションの「バージョン」をクリックし、右の詳細パネルにてタイプを「一般」から「バージョン」に変更します。

2.8. ディメンションの名前を「Version」に変更し、「OK」をクリックします。

2.9. 「レビュー」をクリックします。（バージョンカテゴリをマッピングする必要があるため）

2.10.  Acutalのカテゴリとして「Actuals」、Budgetのカテゴリとして「Budget」を選択して「OK」をクリックします。

2.11. ディメンションの「地域」をクリックし、名前を「Col__3」から「Region」に変更します。同様な処理を「事業セグメント」（Segment）、「製品グループ」（Product）に行います。

2.12. モデルを保存します。左上の保存マークをクリックします。
2.13. 名前を「M_P/Ldata」に変更し、「保存」をクリックします。

3. ストーリーの作成

3.1. ナビゲーションバーの「ストーリー」をクリックし、作成にある「キャンバス」を選択します。

3.2. 「作成」をクリックします。

3.3. 左パネルのアセットの中から「チャート」をドラッグ＆ドロップします。

3.4. 2で作成したモデルを選択します。

3.5. 右のビルダパネルにて、現在選択されているチャートを「数値ポイント」にします。

3.6. メジャーの一次値の「＋少なくとも1メジャーが必要です」をクリックし、「売上高」を選択します。

3.7. 売上高を示すチャートが完成しました。サイズや位置を自由に調整します。

3.8. 左パネルよりチャートをドラッグ＆ドロップし、右のビルダパネルにてメジャーの「＋少なくとも1メジャーが必要です」より「売上高」を、ディメンションの「＋ディメンションの追加」より「事業セグメント」を選択します。

事業セグメントごとの売上高を示す棒チャートが完成しました。

3.9. 作成した棒チャートの「…」＞「コピー」＞「複製」をクリックして、チャートを複製します。

3.10. 複製されたチャートが選択された状態で、ビルダパネルにてディメンションで選択されている「事業セグメント」を削除します。


3.11. 「＋ディメンションの追加」より「Date」を選択し、階層を「レベル3」にします。
四半期ごとの売上高を示す棒チャートが完成しました。

3.12. 左パネルのアセットの中から「テーブル」をドラッグ＆ドロップします。

3.13. 行の「＋ディメンションを追加」より「事業セグメント」と「地域」を選択します。列の「＋ディメンションを追加」より「Date」を追加します。

3.14. テーブル上でDateの「＞」をクリックし、年単位に展開します。

3.15. サイズや位置を整えて完成です。左上の「保存」をクリックします。

3.16. 名前を付けて「保存」をクリックします。

今回はサンプルモデル／ストーリーの作成方法のご紹介でした。SAP Analytics Cloudは様々な表現や便利な機能があり、本ブログシリーズでご紹介しています。
はじめてのSAP Analytics Cloud BI - シリーズまとめ - SAP Community からご参照ください。

entry.title: Re: 外注について
entry.content:
①自社資産に一度計上せずにB加工先の特殊在庫に計上する方法があるか。

No, it is not possible to only take the material as supplier's special stock if this material does not belong to your company's stock.

②MRPにて生成される購買発注の納入先はB加工先としたい、そうするためのマスタ設定（購買情報マスタ etc）はどうなるか。

Do you mean by the PO's delivery address should be the supplier's address instead of plant address. If this is the case, you can input the sub supplier code in the PO's delivery address and mark the SC Supp indicator, so that , the goods receipt is posted direct to the "stock with subcontractor" of this supplier.

entry.title: Re: Every time I try to multiply two different accounts i...
entry.content: Yupp, tried already. Here some Pictures of the problem: Bild In this case I only have the calculation without the weighting based on the first column. My goal for example is to multiply the 800M with the -27M/833M. That Way the total amount calculated is correctly weightet based on last years Budget. Bild In This case I try to multiply in the Data Action the ratio 27M/833M with the valculation itself (800M)

entry.title: Re: SAP Process Automation - Your first simple Action Project: End to End

I am trying to implement a batch call in SAP Build Process Automation using an Action Task. I have explained the complete details in another community post. Please have a look and let me know how I can achieve this functionality.

URL: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/actions-in-sap-build-process-automation-need-to-call-batch-in-action-task/qaq-p/14112402#M4915166

Thanks,
Parth

entry.title: Re: SAP SD
entry.content: I presume, the delivery is already created and saved.  For goods issue, IDoc is processed.  If this is so, would like to know why a different quantity is being passed via IDoc ?  Ideally, delivery quantity and picking quantity should be same. Also, when you say quantity conversion, different UoM maintained in material master ?  What is maintained as Base UoM and Sales UoM ?

entry.title: Re: 999 storage type difference posting for sto
entry.content: You can run the LI21 transaction as a periodic background job if you wish to automate clearing.

The issue is not occurring if i create page from smartedit.

entry.title: SAP WCM PM Configuration
entry.content:
Dear All,

This is related to SAP WCM - Work Permit System in Plant Maintenance.

As per business requirements we need to create more than 9 Applications (work permit) in the WCM.

Can you please help us that this can be possible through configuration or any business function activation.

I am attaching the config screen shot in that mentioned user field, kindly check and suggest further on this.

entry.title: Re: Setting up reporting in SAP Enable Now
entry.content: Hi Anton, that was so easy 🫣Thank you very much!! Heli

entry.title: Re: SAP Business Data Cloud - FAQs
entry.content:
Hello @jeetendrakapase 

very informative Blog. I learned alot going through the questions and answers you provided. However, I feel like there are still gaps in my understanding. To provide compelling and objectively correct marketing content for our existing and potential customers, I would like to close those gaps. I have thus compiled a list of questions, which I hope you can answer.

  1. Is the Object Store, that contains the Data Lake Files, Part of SAP Datasphere? Is the Object Store Part of the Foundation Services? Are the Foundation Services Part of SAP Datasphere?
  2. Are Data Products only referring to the Data Lake Files in the Object Store, or do also activated Objects in SAP Datasphere or SAP Databricks count as Data Products?
  3. Are Insight Apps and Intelligent Applications labelling the same thing?
  4. Do Insight Apps contain Datasets and Workflows within SAP Databricks? Will this be part of future developments?
  5. In regards to SAP BW/4HANA PCE, does the Data Product Generator create objects in the Object Store as Data Lake Files or as activated Objects directly in SAP Datasphere? Will this be supported by Insight Apps?
  6. Is the SAP-managed Space within SAP Datasphere separate from the Foundation Services? Are there only the activated objects of i.e. Insight Apps inside that space?
  7. Joule has access to everything inside of Business Data Cloud, in addition to any SAP Cloud System that is connected. Are these considered Part of SAP BDC? In which Frontends would Joule typically be used in? Are any of those considered Part of SAP BDC?
  8. I have seen the term Data Product Package mentioned a few times. Could Insight Apps be considered a form of Data Product Package? Is there a clear differentiation?
  9. Assuming as a partner I could provide Data Products and Data Product Packages. Is it feasible to curate these Packages like Insight Apps from SAP but have them managed by a partner in a similar fashion?
  10. Will Data Integration primarily take place through the Foundation Services going forward? Or will it be more common to connect directly to SAP Datasphere? Which role do the SAP Cloud Connector and Data Provisioning Agent play?
  11. Is storage of physical data in Datasphere a common occurence going forward? Or will the data storage primarily take place via Data Lake Files in the Object Store?

Thank you in advance and best regards,

Adrian

entry.title: Re: SAP WCM PM Configuration
entry.content:
I won't expect much, but which of the business functions (LOG_EAM_WCM_1–3) have you checked so far?

  • LOG_EAM_WCM_1: Focuses on stepwise tagging and the use of work permits, enabling the management of work clearance documents and applications.
  • LOG_EAM_WCM_2 enhances the ability to define WCM object templates for generating WCM objects assigned to maintenance orders, building on the existing functionality of work clearance document templates.
  • LOG_EAM_WCM_3 provides flexible management of different types of WCM objects, including work approvals and applications, and offers new configuration options for customising these objects.

If none found, consider creation of a ticket/case or even a request in Sap Influence

entry.title: New Pipeline Concept features with version 1.0.12: improved handling of Custom Header Properties
entry.content:
A new version (1.0.12) of the Process Integration Pipeline package has recently been released, featuring several minor technical updates, bug fixes, and a reworked approach to handling custom header properties. This blog outlines the enhancements made in the area of custom header property processing.

Intro

Starting with version 1.0.7 of the Process Integration Pipeline package, support for custom header properties was introduced. Custom header properties enable enhanced searchability of the message processing log by allowing key-value pairs – extracted from the message payload or other relevant attributes – to be indexed and queried.

To use this feature, you must configure the customHeaderProperties header in a scenario-specific integration flow step. This header is then propagated to the generic integration flows. The header value is defined as a pipe-delimited ('|') string, where each key-value pair is separated by a colon (':'). For example:

purchaseOrder:${property.purchaseOrder}|companyCode:${property.companyCode}|vendorNumber:${property.vendorNumber}

This implementation meets basic requirements for transmitting custom header properties across multiple integration flows via a single header. However, it is somewhat error-prone due to the use of fixed delimiter characters. If any value contains reserved characters such as colons (e.g., in timestamps), parsing may fail.

Another limitation is the two-step setup required when extracting multiple key fields from an XML payload using XPath: first, each value must be stored in a message header or exchange property, and only then can they be combined into a single customHeaderProperties string.

To address these limitations, an enhanced processing option has been introduced. Instead of using a delimited string, you can define exchange properties with the prefix 'customHeader_' and invoke the Groovy script addCustomHeaderProperties. This script generates the customHeaderProperties header as a Base64-encoded JSON object, preserving all key-value pairs.

With this approach, special characters within header values – such as colons, pipes, or quotes – are fully supported and correctly handled, making the process more robust and reliable.

New processing option: Using exchange properties

For each custom header property, you can create an exchange property with prefix 'customHeader_' in a content modifier step. The name of the exchange property without the prefix becomes the name of the custom header property.

After the Content Modifier step, you need to invoke the Groovy script addCustomHeaderProperties from the script collection Pipeline Generic - Script Collection, which is part of the provided integration package. This script reads all exchange properties prefixed with 'customHeader_', constructs a JSON object from their name-value pairs, and Base64-encodes the result.

In addition to generating the customHeaderProperties header, the script also attaches these custom header values to the message processing log, ensuring they are available for monitoring and diagnostics. The resulting customHeaderProperties header is then seamlessly passed along to the generic integration flows.

In the generic integration flows, the addCustomHeaderProperties Groovy script splits the header value using the pipe character ('|') for backward compatibility. It then evaluates each resulting segment (or the original value if no pipe is present) to determine whether it is Base64-encoded. All key-value pairs extracted from the decoded or raw segments are stored in a map, which is subsequently used as the source for custom header properties.

In the message monitor, the custom headers are displayed in the Properties tab of the message processing logs of the generic integration flows. In the filter section, you can search for specific custom header values.

Previous Approach Still Supported: Manually Setting customHeaderProperties

In a content modifier step, you can create a new header customHeaderProperties. The source value of the header customHeaderProperties must follow the following pattern:

  • Name and value are separated by colon (':')
  • Name and value pairs are separated by pipe ('|')

Optionally, you can invoke the addCustomHeaderProperties script to add custom header properties to your scenario-specific flow.

At the same time, the script transforms the previously defined pipe-delimited string into a JSON object, encodes it in Base64, and updates the customHeaderProperties header accordingly: {"docType":"Z0","docRefId":"Test Doc 01"}

In the generic integration flows, the Groovy script addCustomHeaderProperties splits up the list of name and value pairs and adds the custom header properties accordingly.

In the message monitor, the custom headers are displayed in the Properties tab of the message processing logs of the generic integration flows. In the filter section, you can search for specific custom header values.

Combined approach

A combined approach is also supported: you can use the customHeaderProperties header with a pipe-delimited string, and simultaneously define exchange properties with the prefix 'customHeader_' in the same Content Modifier.

In the example below, the custom header key companyCode is included both in the delimited string and as an exchange property.

When the addCustomHeaderProperties script is invoked, it merges the entries from the customHeaderProperties header and the 'customHeader_' exchange properties into a single map. It then logs the resulting custom header properties in the message processing log and sets a new merged value – encoded as a Base64 JSON object – back to the customHeaderProperties header.

In this case, the value from the exchange property customHeader_companyCode takes precedence over the one from the delimited string. This is because the script first reads values from the existing header, then adds or overwrites them with those from the exchange properties – allowing you to override specific values if needed.

entry.title: list of standard OCN which is supported by SAP SF.
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

I want a list of Standard OCN's which are supported by SAP SF.

Thanks

entry.title: From idea to app: using AI-powered Project Accelerator to develop SAP Fiori apps
entry.content:
Why use the AI-powered Project Accelerator from SAP Fiori tools?

Imagine you're with colleagues or customers, standing at a whiteboard, sketching the UI of your future SAP Fiori elements app to determine what UI will meet your business requirements.

 

Or maybe you're designing the data model for that app:

 

 

What are the typical next steps to build a working SAP Fiori elements app?

You would normally:

  • Create a data model
  • Prepare the mock data
  • Build an OData service
  • Generate the SAP Fiori app
  • Add the necessary annotations

Now, with the AI-powered Project Accelerator from SAP Fiori tools, you can skip all those steps. Let the AI generate everything for you—including the data model, OData service, sample data, and UI annotations. How much time will that save you in your project? In this post, I’ll explain how to use this powerful new capability, including the steps to set up a trial account and try it for yourself.

Generating a prototype quickly is critical for gathering user feedback

At SAP, we frequently engage with customers in focused co-innovation events. Typically, these involve customers and SAP domain and product experts brainstorming solutions with designers. Then, a couple of dedicated developers invest hours (or even pull all-nighters) to create a prototype for next-day validation and refinement.

These bootcamps are incredibly productive, with every moment of collaboration being invaluable. The desire to expedite review and update cycles by seeing prototypes instantly has always been strong. Now, with the integration of AI technologies into our SAP Fiori tools, this instant prototyping is a reality—first for our internal development teams and now for all SAP customers seeking the same advantages.

In a few minutes, starting with a document, sketch, or other visual representation of your app, you can preview the UI of your list report – object page app.

List Report:

Object Page:

The generated project is fully compliant with SAP Fiori tools and can be deployed as a productive application when complete.

 

Ready to try out the AI-powered Project Accelerator?

 

Get started with either a paid SAP Build Code subscription or a free basic trial account.

Setup Steps with SAP Build Code subscription

You’ll need an SAP BTP account with an SAP Build Code subscription. Create a Full-Stack Dev Space containing both the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP) and SAP Fiori tools extensions.

Setup Steps with a free basic trial.

Alternatively, use a free basic trial of SAP Build Code.
More details here:
👉SAP Trial Developer Tools

Note: the free basic trial may have limited quota for AI scenarios.

Click Try Now to generate your trial user ID and password.

Create Your First Project

  1. Log in to your SAP Build Code basic trial account.

  2. Open the Basic Trial – SAP Build Code Workbook.

  3. Follow the steps in Unit 2 – Create Project to create your Full-Stack Dev Space.

  4. At Step 6, personalize:

    • Project name (e.g., travels)

    • Description (e.g., Fiori Tools AI)

    • Dev space name (e.g., tryFioriToolsAI)

📌You do not need to complete any other chapters in the workbook.

Use the Project Accelerator

  1. In SAP Build Code, right-click in the left panel and select SAP Fiori.

  2. Open the Project Accelerator section.

The Project Accelerator is the AI component of SAP Fiori Tools that connects to a large language model (LLM) on SAP BTP. It automatically generates all artifacts for your application.

In the Application Requirements section, provide your business requirements.

Alternatively, you can use /fiori-gen-spec-app command in Joule. It will recognize your request and redirect you to the Project Accelerator:

 

Provide Your Business Requirements

As an input, you can use:

  • Text descriptions (Example 2: Display Customers with Related Contracts)

  • Images (Example: Manage Travel List Report Page). An image can represent a single page UI corresponding to the List Report Page or Object Page floorplan or a data model.

  • Combination of text and images (Example: Manage Travel App). If you want to add multiple images representing different app pages, you should use the combination of text and images, explaining in the text what the images referrer to.

Prepare user requirements as outlined above. For example, download an image of the data model from this blog, then:

  1. Open your workspace folder in SAP Build Code:

    • Go to File > Open Folder...

    • Select /home/user/projects/

  2. Create a folder under "Projects" (e.g., Requirements).

  3. Upload the image:

    • Right-click the folder and select Upload…

 

4. Switch to SAP Fiori from Explorer.

5. In Project Accelerator, click Choose File and select the uploaded image.

Then, click Generate.

 

Preview Your App

In a few minutes, the app will appear in the staging area with two options:

  • Accept Project

  • Preview

Feel free to review the code in the generated artefacts.

You can also select Preview to launch the CAP server and view the app in a new browser tab.

You’ll see an SAP Fiori elements app with:

  • A List Report (filter bar + table of travels)

  • A Travel Object Page

  • A Booking Detail Page (second object page)

 

 

If the generated app meets your needs, press Accept to move it to your dev space.

You can then use SAP Fiori tools to adjust your application UI. For more information on using SAP Fiori tools, see Getting Started with SAP Fiori tools  

Additional Resources

  • Supported Features in Application Requirements

  • Generating an App with the Project Accelerator or Joule

  • 🎥Watch the video: Project Accelerator: Elevate Development with AI in SAP Fiori tools

entry.title: Top 10 SAP Cloud ALM News May 2025
entry.content:
Welcome to the May edition of our Top 10 News in SAP Cloud ALM!

This month, we're thrilled to bring you a variety of exciting updates and enhancements designed to streamline your SAP Cloud ALM experience. We've been diligently working to introduce new features and improve existing ones to help you manage your implementation, operations, and transformation processes more efficiently.

Let's dive in and explore these updates in detail!

 

IMPLEMENTATION UPDATES

1. Streamline Document Creation with Templates

Enhance your document management process by utilizing templates to maintain a consistent structure across your files. This feature allows you to create new documents quickly and efficiently, saving you time and effort. To learn more about how to create and use templates for your documents, please refer to the Application Help section on Creating Documents.

 

2. Solution Process Viewer Now Displays Assigned Interfaces

When you call up the details for a solution process in the Solution Process Viewer, you can now see information about any interfaces that are assigned to it.  As well as the name of any assigned interfaces, you can also see their respective IDs and a  icon, representing the Process Authoring app as the origin of where these interface library types were created. 

 

Note

The Interfaces section is currently only shown if corresponding assignments exist.     

 

 

3. Enhanced Process Assignment Search

In this release, we have introduced a more efficient way to assign requirements, user stories, and tasks to your solution processes. When assigning these items, you can now easily identify them by their unique IDs, which are displayed alongside their titles. Additionally, we have implemented a powerful search functionality that allows you to quickly find the desired items by searching for specific keywords within their titles, IDs, or the titles of their associated scopes.

 

4. Interface Relationship Management

Simplify interface organization and establish clear relationships between interfaces using the new feature in Libraries. With the ability to assign and unassign interfaces to other interfaces, you can now create a hierarchical structure that reflects the dependencies and connections within your design system. This feature enables you to maintain a clean and organized interface library, making it easier to navigate and understand the relationships between various interface elements.

 

5. Enhanced Requirement Traceability Data Export with PDF Support

The Requirement Distribution app now offers a convenient and efficient way to export requirement traceability data in PDF format. This new feature enables users to generate comprehensive reports that include a professional cover page, a well-organized table of contents and detailed information about each requirement. This functionality is beneficial for users who need to share or archive traceability data in a standardized and easily accessible format.

 

 

OPERATIONS UPDATES

6. Improved Business Service Management with Planned Availability Events

Business Service Management now supports Planned Availability events for private cloud systems. These events, which are either created by SAP or in the Event Calendar, represent up-time reservations required for service requests. With the introduction of Planned Availability events, you can now configure Event Actions specifically tailored to this event type. For more detailed information on Planned Availability events, please refer to the Event Types documentation.

 

7. Quick Access to Preferred Managed Components with Favorites

The new Favorites feature allows you to mark your preferred managed components, making them easily accessible from the Favorites section on the home page. Favorites are displayed regardless of the selected scope, enabling quick navigation to frequently used services or systems without the need to define the scope each time. To add a service or system as a favorite, choose the required components in  (Select a Scope), apply the selection, and then select  (Add to Favorites) on the card of the preferred service or system in the Home Overview section. For more information on using favorites, refer to the Application Help.

 

 

RISE WITH SAP UPDATES

8. SAP Readiness Check Key Results Card Available in System View

The SAP Readiness Check Key Results card, previously known as the Upgrade Assessment card, is now available under the Software Stack category in System View. This enhanced card provides an overview of the most critical findings from the latest SAP Readiness Check for SAP S/4HANA Upgrades run on your system. It helps you assess your readiness to upgrade your system or estimate the effort required for the upgrade by displaying key results such as the number of relevant simplification items, custom code findings, impacted remote interfaces, and more (details displayed on screenshot below). Click on "Latest Result" to view the details of the last SAP Readiness Check run or choose "New Analysis" to initiate a new check for your system.

For more information, see SAP Readiness Check and Software Stack on the SAP Help Portal.

 

 

BUSINESS TRANSFORMATION CENTER UPDATES

9. Expanded Cycle Restart Capabilities

The cycle and monitoring feature has been enhanced to provide more flexibility in restarting cycles. Users now have the ability to restart both the cycle preparation and cycle transformation processes, even when they are already in progress. This includes scenarios where the status is "In Progress with Error".

 

10. New “Associated Cycles” Tab in Transformation Model Version App

The Transformation Model Version app has been updated with a new feature to improve transparency and traceability. Users can now easily view all cycles that have been derived from a specific transformation model version in the newly added "Associated Cycles" tab. This tab provides a comprehensive list of all cycles that have been created based on the selected transformation model version.

 

 

-----------------------------------------------

Stay tuned for future editions where we’ll continue to bring you the latest innovations and improvements in SAP Cloud ALM and explore the SAP Help Portal!

Together, we can enhance your SAP Cloud ALM experience and unlock new horizons of productivity and success. Embrace the opportunity, and happy transforming! 🚀

entry.title: Is it common to use SAP BAS Productivity Tools in real business cases for CAP developement?
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I'm currently following the SAP Learning Journey "Developing Full-Stack Applications Using Productivity Tools in SAP Business Application Studio". I find the tools quite intuitive and useful in a learning context, however, based on my experience across a few real-world CAP projects, I’ve rarely (if ever) seen these tools actually used in practice. Most teams I've worked with tend to rely on manual coding without touching the productivity features.

I'd really appreciate hearing about your experience or your team’s approach, especially if you've used these tools in production environments.

Thanks in advance!

You want to know if it's possible to mass load G/L account through Managa Operating Chart of Accounts app, isn't it ?

Thank you in advance for your feedback.

Jeremy

1. 2 storage location belongs to a new MRP area( type:02 )

2. Set a indicator for the (without storage location check) in OVZ9

3. when create SO should entry the storage location

entry.title: Item was saved inconsistently in PMR
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

When I running /SCWM/PMR for PMR in Embedded EWM, the system showed the error "Item was saved inconsistently". 

the Item status:

The Item detail:

Could you please give me some suggestion? 

Regards

Vincent

entry.title: Re: How to fetch compound employee record based on 1 fiel...
entry.content:
Hello,

Since the interface frequency is monthly, I think the queryMode can be periodDelta instead of Delta. This will not only give you records that have changed since your lastrun but will also return you records that have a Start_Date within your given range.

As far as the previous tags are concerned, you will only get the previous tags when a certain field is Changed.  In case of INSERT you won't get the previous tags as INSERT action indicates that its a new records creation. 

Now coming to the logic that might work for you would be as below:

IF action == 'CHANGE' AND custom_String19 != custom_String19_previous AND custom_String19_previous != '' then consider that record. 

IF action == 'INSERT' AND custom_string19 exists (custom_string19) then consider the record. 

Let me know if this works for you.

Thanks,

Pranav.

entry.title: Re: Selective Data Transition with Time Slice.
entry.content: Thanks

entry.title: BW: Parallel process not use BTC job of SM50 ?
entry.content:
Hi experts,

I set the number of DTP Parallel processes equal to '10'. And the available BTC job of SM50 is 20.

But the packages of DTP is 3 after running.

Why the number of packages is less than the setting of Parallel processes ?

entry.title: Re: I'm not able to access Process Manager from SAP Signa...
entry.content: Hi all, sorry, I cannot answer to Mikes last reply. It Seems that you have not created an own trial workspace but you are in an SAP internal existing workspace and there you did not have sufficient licenses/rights assigned. Please create a ticket for the product support team internally via SAP for Me and ask for an own workspace (internally you can also get more than 30 days) via https://me.sap.com/notes/3319041. All the best, Dana

entry.title: Can the extended withholding tax be determined based on only one of several expense items?
entry.content:
Hello community,

I got a question regarding the functionality of the extended withholding tax function.

The help portal states that the number of withholding taxes for each document item is not limited to one.

How exactly is this to be understood?

Specifically, my question is about postings in which multiple expense items on one invocie are posted to a single vendor, whereby withholding tax is only incurred on one of the expense items of the invocie.

This might look like the following:

Expenses for goods €500 (not subject to withholding tax) & expenses for licence fees €1000 (subject to withholding tax) to vendor account.

In such cases, can the extended withholding tax function determine and post withholding tax based on only the licence expenses, or is the withholding tax base necessarily the total invoice amount / amount of all expenses?

Thanks in advance for your comments.

entry.title: Joule answers in eclipse
entry.content:
What is the best way to deal with wrong answers from joule?

I have asked for a program to write table entries to a transport. It is not bad, but it has a few bugs. e.g. a function module is proposed that does not exist.

After I wrote that it does not exist, another one was suggested to me that does not exist.

I could simply change the program and use it, which would be much faster than if I created it myself. But perhaps I could get a correct answer in the future with a few prompts? At the moment, however, this takes longer than correcting it in the coding.

entry.title: Release of the New Scenario SAP Readiness Check for SAP Supply Chain Solutions
entry.content:
As SAP Advanced Planning and Optimization (SAP APO) is a component of SAP Supply Chain Management (SAP SCM), and SAP SCM 7.0 will reach the end of mainstream maintenance in the coming years, customers need to plan and migrate from SAP APO to SAP Integrated Business Planning (SAP IBP), leverage the integrated supply chain planning functionality within SAP S/4HANA, or a combination of both.

Today, we proudly announce the general availability of SAP Readiness Check for SAP Supply Chain solutions to assist in planning this transformation. This new SAP Readiness Check scenario provides customers and their partners with insightful information on how SAP APO is used today.

By analysing active SAP APO modules, SAP Readiness Check for SAP Supply Chain solutions supports the definition of the target solution architecture.

SAP Readiness Check for SAP Supply Chain solutions provides customers, implementation partners, and SAP consultants visibility on how the existing SAP APO modules, like SAP APO Demand Planning, SAP APO Supply Network Planning, SAP APO Production Planning and Detailed Scheduling, and Global Available-to-Promise, are implemented, tailored, and consumed.

Unlike the existing verification points presented by SAP Readiness Check for SAP S/4HANA, where the checks reveal “must-dos” in support of a possible conversion project to SAP S/4HANA, SAP Readiness Check for SAP Supply Chain solutions is purely informative and is intended to be used as input for discussions between customers, their partners, and SAP to identify the best-fitting target architecture, as well as to help scope initial effort estimations.

What is SAP Integrated Business Planning (SAP IBP)?

The SAP Integrated Business Planning (SAP IBP) solution is a cloud-based supply chain planning solution that scales to accommodate business growth and integrates with other SAP and third-party systems. It integrates key aspects of the planning process, including demand, supply, inventory, and sales and operations planning (S&OP). Designed to help you streamline planning and improve performance, SAP IBP supports data-driven decision-making and simplified collaboration to increase operational efficiency. SAP IBP delivers this with the help of advanced analytics, artificial intelligence, and powerful optimisation algorithms that enable real-time visibility, better forecasting, and faster decision-making across the entire supply chain.
For further information, check the SAP Integrated Business Planning Community.

Supply chain planning functionality available in SAP S/4HANA

  • SAP S/4HANA ePPDS
    SAP S/4HANA offers Embedded PP/DS, which brings the SAP APO capabilities into the SAP ERP core. Embedded PP/DS brings many advantages, such as simplified master data maintenance, simplified integration, and planning PP/DS materials in MRP Live.
    The major innovation, however, is related to the user interface with SAP Fiori apps for capacity monitoring and capacity leveling. The SAP Fiori app Monitor Capacity Utilization provides an overview of the capacity load in the resources assigned to the area of responsibility and a detailed capacity overview of a selected resource, including charts and additional details of the capacity load.
    For further information, see S/4HANA Embedded PP/DS: Shift Maintenance in Monitor Capacity Fiori App. | SAP Blogs
  • SAP S/4HANA aATP
    Advanced Available-to-Promise (aATP) is a business function in SAP S/4HANA that responds to order fulfillment requests in Sales and Production Planning.
    An order fulfillment request consists of the required material, plant, requested quantity, and material availability date. The response to the request is based on the current stock situation,  future stock receipts, and real-time orders.
    Furthermore, additional restrictions can be applied based on other order attributes (like region or customers). The ATP function generates confirmation proposals for the requested material and plant, including confirmed quantities and dates.
    For further information, see Advanced Available-to-Promise (aATP) | SAP Help Portal.

 

SAP Readiness Check for SAP Supply Chain solutions

Overview
The dashboard contains the following three tiles:

  • Master Data: Shows a summary of active SAP SCM master data components, their identified dependent levels, and the corresponding data volumes in the analyzed system.
  • Key Data by Application Area: Provides insights into the key data of the different SCM components used in the analyzed system. This analysis can help to define the target system architecture and the main processes to be activated.
  • System Configuration Settings: Offers a summary of the main settings active in the analyzed system to identify requirements for the future solution architecture.

Master Data
The Master Data tile provides the top 6 objects, while the detail view provides insights into all active objects.

The detail view shows the active master data objects and the number of entries, together with additional details like table name, number of objects created last year, and number of user-created objects over the past year. The table can be exported, including all the detailed information provided.

Key Data by Application Area
This tile provides insights into the key data of the different SAP SCM components used within the analyzed system. This analysis helps to define the target system architecture by highlighting the scope and scale of the active capabilities, thus delivering input to the complexity of the data migration project.

The detail view shows the application areas in use for:

  • Demand Planning (DP)
  • Supply Planning (SNP)
  • Capable-to-Match (CTM)
  • Global Available-to-Promise (GATP)
  • Production Planning and Detailed Scheduling (PP/DS)
  • APO Documents

The object level detail helps define what data may need to be replicated to the target SAP Supply Chain solution.

System Configuration Settings
This tile provides an immediate high-level overview of the scenarios used within the system and the relevance of the analyzed objects, supporting the identification of recommended target solutions.

The following configuration settings are considered during the SAP Readiness Check analysis:

  • Type of planning used (Building Block / Campaign / Bottleneck / pull capacity planning / Trimming)
  • Used statistical models for demand generation
  • Used heuristic planning profiles
  • Used planning areas for each module
  • Planning strategy used, including customized configuration
  • Global configuration information referred to module GATP (Global-Available-to-Promise)
  • Distribution configuration information
  • Layout of the delivery network
  • Customized key figures and attributes in the system
  • Feature-based prediction settings
  • Number of documents managed in APO

 

Getting Started
To set up and run SAP Readiness Check for SAP Supply Chain solutions, the subsequent steps need to be performed:
Follow the instructions in SAP Note 3477050 - SAP Readiness Check for SAP Supply Chain solutions to install the check framework and transport the changes into your target system, such as the productive client of the production system. The note provides detailed and technical information on how to set up and perform the analysis.

Initiate program RC_SCM_COLLECT_ANALYSIS_DATA via transaction SA38 or SE38 to initialize the data collectors.

Select the scope of the analysis by activating or deactivating the corresponding checkboxes. Use the Schedule Analysis button to schedule the data collection. The process runs as a background job to avoid timeout issues.

The runtime of the data collectors depends on many factors, like server capacity, data volumes, and component usage. Therefore, it is difficult to predict the runtime. The data collection process was intentionally designed to avoid contention or interference with production workloads; consequently, you may see longer than expected runtimes. The best way to forecast the runtime for a production system is to conduct the data collection process in a copy of production using a similar hardware configuration.

Once the analysis has finished, you can download the results to your local filesystem using the Download Analysis Data button. The generated data collection archive is a ZIP file containing multiple XML files. All collected data is human-readable and can be easily inspected with appropriate XML viewers.

Launch the SAP Readiness Check cloud application on SAP for Me or from within your SAP Cloud ALM tenant and choose Start New Analysis to upload the ZIP archive. After a few seconds, the results of the newly created SAP Readiness Check analysis can be explored.

As always, we look forward to receiving your feedback!
Your SAP Readiness Check Team

entry.title: Country Specific field for Switzerland missing from the Supplier Invoicing API
entry.content:
For supplier invoices in Switzerland, the QR_IBAN field is necessary. Is there a corresponding field used for QR-IBAN when generating supplier invoices through the API?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Country Specific field for Switzerland missing from t...
entry.content:
You can refer to the below help documentation regarding supplier invoice creation with QR_IBAN.
Create Supplier Invoice with QR-IBAN
Sample Payload for Posting a Supplier Invoice with QR-IBAN in Supplier Invoice - Create (A2X, Inbound, Synchronous)

entry.title: Understanding "My Processes" and "My Requests" in SBPA: Empowering Users Through Clarity and Control
entry.content:
SAP Build Process Automation is a low-code/no-code platform by SAP that enables businesses to automate workflows and business processes quickly and with minimal programming effort. 

As organizations increasingly adopt automation to streamline business workflows, user-centric features become essential for both transparency and efficiency. Two key views that support this in SAP Build Process Automation are: 
“My Processes” 
“My Requests” 

While they may sound similar, they serve distinct purposes in helping users and teams monitor and interact with automated processes. Let’s explore what they are, why they matter, and how they enhance the overall automation experience. 

 

Problem Statement: 

As organizations adopt workflow automation to streamline operations, users often struggle to track and manage their interactions with automated systems. Despite automation improving execution speed and consistency, lack of transparency and user feedback remains a major issue. 

 

The Problem: 

Users currently lack intuitive, centralized views within SAP Build Process Automation that clearly separate and present (a) the complex end-to-end business processes they participate in and (b) their individual service or task requests—leading to confusion, inefficient follow-ups, and reduced trust in automation. 

This results in: 

  • Uncertainty about the current status of submitted requests (e.g., approvals, service tickets, form submissions) 
  • Inability to monitor progress or identify delays in multi-step business processes 
  • Increased support queries to help desks or process owners asking for updates 
  • Low adoption or engagement with self-service automation tools due to poor visibility 

 

Why It Matters: 

  • Business users need a “My Requests” view to track simple, individual submissions quickly. 
  • Power users or process participants need a “My Processes” view to monitor end-to-end workflows they’re involved in or responsible for. 

 

The Need: 

There is a clear need for two distinct, yet complementary, views: 

  • “My Requests” – to provide users with a simple interface for tracking their personal submissions and their current statuses. 
  • “My Processes” – to allow users to oversee, participate in, and manage the lifecycle of complex, automated workflows. 

 

What are "My Processes" Views? 

“My Processes” application is dedicated to Process Participants like Approvers for example. This application would help the Process Participants easily track, understand, and make informed decisions about where their input is needed and how it fits into the larger process.   

 

Key Use Cases: 

  • Tracking the status of an employee onboarding process 
  • Monitoring a multi-level purchase approval 
  • Managing a customer service workflow 

 

Benefits: 

  • Real-time insight into the process lifecycle 
  • Accountability by showing who is responsible for each task 
  • Faster issue resolution when processes stall or bottleneck 
  • Ideal for process owners, managers, and workflow participants 

 

What are "My Requests" Views? 

"My Requests” View provides clarity to the Requestors on how their request fits into the workflow, the status of their request, and what actions (if any) they need to take as their request moves through the approval or fulfilment process.   

 

Key Use Cases: 

  • Viewing a submitted IT support ticket 
  • Checking status of a travel reimbursement 
  • Tracking a leave request form 

 

Benefits: 

  • Self-service tracking—no need to email or call for updates 
  • Simple, clean interface focused on the user's submissions 
  • Encourages user engagement with automation without needing to understand complex workflows 

 

Key Differences 

Feature 

My Processes 

My Requests 

Focus 

Entire process lifecycle 

Individual request status 

User Role 

Participant or process owner 

Requester or submitter 

Complexity 

Often involves multiple steps 

Usually single or few steps 

 

Prerequisites 

Here are a few Prerequisites for My Processes and My Requests View. 

 

Let’s explore the configuration details:

Configure My Processes Application 

Configure My Requests 

Now that both My Requests and My Processes tiles have been configured, let’s look at how we can visualise them: 

 

Entry Screen: 

 

My Requests view provides an overview of at all the requests initiated by you based on the filter criteria. You also have the possibility to look at the details of each request individually, the stage at which the request currently is in, the history of the previous steps, the status of the steps and the details of the approvers. You also have the possibility to look at all the tasks under a sub-process step (even if there is a hierarchy) and gain insights, without moving away from the lead process and much more. 

 My Requests

 

My Processes application provides an overview of all the processes that you have been given access to. You can also have a look at details of individual process instances and have the same capabilities as in “My Requests” view.  

 My Processes

Additional Key features are listed here. 

 

Conclusion 

As more businesses embrace automation, features like “My Processes” and “My Requests” ensure that automation remains accessible, visible, and actionable. By making it easier to track, monitor, and engage with automated processes, SAP Build Process Automation delivers not just technical power—but real business value. 

 

More Information: 

Configure My Processes Application 

Configure My Requests 

Use My Processes Application 

SAP BTP Innboytes blog post

 

This is only the beginning - stay tuned!

entry.title: Зустрічаємося на головній події фінансових директорів України
entry.content:
Ексклюзивна знижка на Ukrainian CFO Forum від партнера SAP - Business Evolution 

Запрошуємо вас на головну подію року для фінансових директорів із ексклюзивною знижкою 20% та можливістю зустрітися з командою SAP Ukraine.

Форум Фінансових Директорів України – головна щорічна подія у сфері корпоративних фінансів, що з 1997 року об’єднує C-level керівників провідних компаній для обміну досвідом, аналізу викликів і формування стратегій сталого розвитку. Єдиний в Україні форум такого масштабу за глибиною експертизи й рівнем учасників.

Де?

- Київ та онлайн

Коли?

- Brave CFO Gathering — 6 червня 2025

- Ukrainian CFO Forum — 23–24 жовтня 2025
Більше про події тут.

 

Серед спікерів — наш партнер Олексюк Андрій, CCO Business Evolution. Вже 6 червня він поділиться практичним досвідом у виступі на тему: «Нові технології оптимізації ключових бізнес-процесів: співпраця фінансового та ІТ-директора».

🎯Як отримати знижку 20%

  • Використайте промокод BraveBE при реєстрації  
  • Знижка поширюється на всі типи квитків
  • Промокод діє до 31 січня 2025 

Побачимось на форумі😉

entry.title: Re: 'IAM APP' does not appear in eclipse
